items.title: Rethinking Community Health Work In Malaysia — June Joseph
items.summary: While volunteerism can support engagement, it cannot deliver sustained care at scale. A tiered, professionalised community health workforce offers a way forward that preserves trust while strengthening system reliability. The post Rethinking Community Health Work In Malaysia — June Joseph appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Emerging Asia May Face Increased Food Cost Pressures: Fitch Ratings
items.summary: Fitch Ratings warns of rising cost pressure across agribusinesses and food supply chains amid reduced fertiliser supply and higher prices, especially in countries that rely on imported food. Food import dependence is 10% for Malaysia, a net food importer. The post Emerging Asia May Face Increased Food Cost Pressures: Fitch Ratings appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Ampang Hospital’s IT System Down For 33 Hours In First Huge Outage
items.summary: The eHIS system at Ampang Hospital, once a model IT hospital, was down for 33 hours in its first massive outage. The IT system, not upgraded since 2007, previously faced frequent failures last year. Ampang Hospital computers run on Windows XP and use IE. The post Ampang Hospital’s IT System Down For 33 Hours In First Huge Outage appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Ex-UMMC Radiographer To File Negligence Suit Over Alleged Radiation Exposure
items.summary: A former UMMC radiographer who developed stage 4 lymphoma plans to sue for negligence, alleging SOP breaches. So far, she is the sole claimant from the so-called cancer "cluster" in her proposed suit. She was deemed unfit last year to continue working. The post Ex-UMMC Radiographer To File Negligence Suit Over Alleged Radiation Exposure appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Autism: Turning Awareness Into Acceptance And Action — OKU Card Holder
items.summary: The government must urgently amend Articles 8(2) and 12(1) of the Federal Constitution to explicitly include “disability” as a prohibited ground of discrimination. The post Autism: Turning Awareness Into Acceptance And Action — OKU Card Holder appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Bukit Gasing Rep Wants Unconditional Coverage Of Pre-Existing Conditions Under Base MHIT
items.summary: Bukit Gasing rep Rajiv Rishyakaran demands unconditional coverage of pre-existing conditions under Base MHIT, not just "stable" cases. He asks what percentage of the adult population living with chronic disease would qualify for the health insurance plan. The post Bukit Gasing Rep Wants Unconditional Coverage Of Pre-Existing Conditions Under Base MHIT appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: France-Led ‘Healthier Food’ Model In Malaysia Targets Value Over Price
items.summary: France-backed Biru-Putih-Hati eggs and meat in Malaysia cost about 20% more, but cut waste and improve nutrition from better animal feed. Uptake hinges on consumer trust and wider availability, as local diets shift toward processed foods and eating out. The post France-Led ‘Healthier Food’ Model In Malaysia Targets Value Over Price appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Malaysian EMS Provider Marks Sixth Asia-Pacific Award Since 2020
items.summary: First Ambulance Services has been named Ambulance Service of the Year (Asia-Pacific) at the Global Health Asia-Pacific Healthcare Awards for the sixth time since 2020. The post Malaysian EMS Provider Marks Sixth Asia-Pacific Award Since 2020 appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Beyond Maternal Health: Rethinking Women’s Health Policy
items.summary: Malaysia has built strong foundations in maternal and reproductive health. The next step is to build on those foundations. This includes linking prevention, screening, and long-term management of chronic conditions alongside reproductive health services. The post Beyond Maternal Health: Rethinking Women’s Health Policy appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Too Many Agencies Involved In Doctor Workforce Pipeline: Health DG
items.summary: DG Mahathar says the doctor workforce pipeline is fragmented across MOHE, JPA, and MOH, causing delays and staff shortages despite adequate workforce numbers. He wants end-to-end workforce planning, competency-based approaches, and less "regulatory drag". The post Too Many Agencies Involved In Doctor Workforce Pipeline: Health DG appeared first on CodeBlue .
